stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses a significant bottleneck in electronics hardware design, which is currently manual and expertise-intensive.
Overcomes key challenges related to developing LLM-suited representations and acquiring large-scale datasets for PCB generation.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels, designed for visual generative modeling.
The dataset is highly accessible, featuring 100M training examples, and crucially, all images are permissively licensed for research and commercial use.
GPIC is a high-quality resource, having undergone safety filtering and deduplication, making it valuable for large-scale AI training.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating its primary function in NLP tasks.
The model utilizes key ML technologies including transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents, enabling AI functionality directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is running AI agents locally, emphasizing privacy and self-hosting.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard models.
These new models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces LLMSurgeon, a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's pretraining corpus.
Addresses the critical, yet often undisclosed, nature of LLM pretraining data ('digital DNA') which dictates model behavior.
Formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to enable post-hoc auditing and understanding of LLM data provenance from generated text.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a fundamental failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global logical consistency.
Introduces a formal metric, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime-computable method to measure the deviation of the agent's output from a theoretically coherent state.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies.
The framework integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to analyze the hidden pretraining data composition of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research addresses the lack of transparency regarding LLM 'digital DNA' (training data mixture), which dictates model behavior.
The method allows researchers to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only its generated text.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses a significant industry bottleneck: PCB schematic design is currently manual and highly expertise-intensive.
Overcomes key challenges in the field, specifically the lack of an LLM-suited representation and large-scale datasets for this task.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) for Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a new method designed to make TTFT practical by improving efficiency.
The core technical approach involves using Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching to balance the trade-off between speed and quality in prompt adaptation.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: the ability to filter poor hypotheses before resource-intensive experimentation.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 real machine learning research proposals sourced from ICLR submissions.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the critical lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The work formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS),' a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's training corpus using only its generated text.
This research enables post-hoc auditing of LLM data provenance and composition, which is crucial for understanding model behaviors and failure modes.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language descriptions.
The research addresses a significant gap in hardware design, as PCB schematic generation is currently manual and expertise-intensive.
The core technical hurdle addressed is developing an LLM-suited representation and acquiring a large-scale dataset for this domain.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation: the lack of realistic, structured clinical benchmarks.
Introduces MedCase-Structured, a new dataset pipeline that generates FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text.
Aims to enable controllable and clinically realistic benchmarking of diagnostic reasoning in EHR settings.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
A key feature is the ability to run these personal AI agents entirely on local, private hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models without cloud reliance.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is under scrutiny for its handling of zero-day exploits.
A figure named 'Nightmare Eclipse' is publicly demonstrating exploit code against Microsoft.
The focus of the reporting is on Microsoft's response to these public disclosures.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The botnet's infrastructure was reportedly linked to a residential proxy network based in Russia.
This represents a significant cybersecurity takedown operation.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude models) in scientific software development, specifically in physics.
A physicist supervised the AI over 12 days to build a differentiable perturbation theory module (CLAX-PT) in JAX.
The research quantifies supervision events, showing the AI autonomously resolved many tasks, supplemented by physicist domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models.
Focuses on optimizing the memory and latency associated with the KV cache in long-rollout causal video diffusion.
Replaces traditional per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent attention mechanism (MLA).

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in applying large Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to time-series anomaly detection.
Highlights that existing benchmarks lack natural-language rationales, hindering model interpretability.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to enable grounded and interpretable anomaly detection decisions.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in LLM reasoning where intermediate steps are tied to autoregressive token generation.
It proposes mimicking human cognition by using internal 'working memory' to manipulate information without externalizing thoughts.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from external communication to enhance LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: New research proposes an alternative to traditional Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for improving frontier reasoning models.
The method involves sampling from a 'power distribution' derived from the base language model, achieving comparable reasoning without extra training or datasets.
A key technical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately 'mix' into this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in conventional Federated Learning (FL) aggregation, which uses fixed weights.
Proposes the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to improve fairness and stability in distributed learning.
Focuses on handling heterogeneous and privacy-sensitive data across multiple clients.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to Reinforcement Learning (RL) for improving frontier reasoning models.
It suggests using a 'power distribution' derived from the base model to elicit comparable reasoning without extra training or datasets.
The main technical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
It proposes 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to generalize to unseen preference domains without extensive retraining.
This work aims to improve the robustness of LLM alignment by moving beyond fixed, single-reward model constraints.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, where intermediate steps are tied to external token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition to use internal 'working memory' for information manipulation.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from the autoregressive generation process to improve LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global consistency.
It introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
This residual is computable at runtime using system output and defined cross-component coupling constraints.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a method for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The model addresses the limitation of existing methods by considering both citation context (prior work direction) and formal structure dependencies.
Aims to create mathematically grounded claims that are both motivated by prior research and structurally valid.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new method suggests that reasoning capabilities in large language models can be achieved by sampling from a 'power distribution' of the base model, bypassing traditional RL fine-tuning.
This approach offers a potential alternative to expensive post-training methods like Reinforcement Learning, reducing reliance on curated datasets and verifiers.
The practical implementation hinges on developing an efficient sampler capable of accurately drawing samples from this specialized power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings fail to meet conventional resolution targets.
Specific findings show unresolved comparisons in both the Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and MMLU-Pro, indicating potential instability in current benchmarks.
The research frames paired LLM evaluation as a hypothesis testing problem, suggesting a need for more robust evaluation methodologies.

stories.title: I Made My AI Models Argue, Then Let Hermes Be the Judge
stories.author: arqamwd
bullet_points: Presents a novel 'multi-model decision agent' architecture.
Utilizes a debate mechanism where three LLMs argue a point.
Introduces a judging LLM (Hermes) to evaluate the debate and determine trustworthiness.

stories.title: Claude vs Gemini Across 4 Security Domains: A Dead Heat — and the Hardening 63% of AI Code Skips
stories.author: ofri-peretz
bullet_points: A comparative security analysis pits Claude against Gemini across four specific security domains.
The models showed parity in JWT and MongoDB security, with Gemini demonstrating an edge in the NestJS round.
A critical finding is that both frontier models exhibit a shared vulnerability related to negative space static analysis, indicating a common gap in AI-generated code hardening.

stories.title: Bringing Scientific Rigor to LLM Comparison
stories.author: lavellehatcherjr
bullet_points: Introduces a CLI tool designed for rigorous comparison of Large Language Models (LLMs).
Features advanced statistical methods like bootstrap CIs and McNemar's test for robust evaluation.
Provides practical utility by including hallucination detection and cost tracking across 8 providers.

stories.title: Is Your Agent Skill Actually Good? Microsoft's Dual-Paper Deep Dive into Skill Evaluation and Self-Evolving Optimization
stories.author: wonderlab
bullet_points: The article addresses the critical problem of evaluating the actual effectiveness of AI agent skills.
It features a deep dive from Microsoft, suggesting a focus on robust skill evaluation methodologies.
The topic touches upon self-evolving optimization, indicating advanced research in AI agent design.

stories.title: How AI reads your website, and what that means for the people who build it
stories.author: onecaratlabs
bullet_points: AI agents are changing how web content is interpreted, potentially altering the intended rendering of websites.
The article discusses the tension between original website structure (HTML) and AI-driven reader views.
It explores the implications for web developers as AI integration becomes more common.
